title: Houses for rent near the RTA Red Line
metaTitle: Houses for Rent Near the RTA Red Line, Cleveland
metaDescription: Looking for houses for rent near the RTA Red Line in Cleveland? See the route, stations, rent ranges, and voucher-friendly homes near the corridor.
primaryKeyword: houses for rent near rta red line cleveland
secondaryKeywords: houses near red line cleveland ohio
rta red line rental homes cleveland
commuter houses near tower city cleveland
answerBox: Greater Cleveland RTA's Red Line is the region's only heavy-rail line, running from Cleveland Hopkins Airport through Downtown's Tower City hub to Windermere in East Cleveland, with stops including Ohio City and University Circle. We work with rental homes across Greater Cleveland — tell us which stretch of the line matters for your commute.

sections.h2: Are there houses for rent near the RTA Red Line?
body: The Red Line runs a single corridor from Cleveland Hopkins International Airport, through Downtown Cleveland's Tower City hub, out to Windermere Station in East Cleveland — so "near the Red Line" can mean several different parts of the city depending on which stretch of the route you want. We work with rental homes across Greater Cleveland; the most useful next step is telling us which end of the line (or which station) matters most for your commute, so we can point you to current listings within a reasonable walk or short drive.
[Book a free showing](/p/book-showing) and share your target station or commute time, and we'll tell you which of our current homes actually fit.

sections.h2: The Red Line route, end to end
body: The Red Line is Greater Cleveland RTA's heavy-rail line, and it's the only rapid-transit line in the United States that connects a downtown directly to its city's main airport — a documented distinction for the system. Trains run roughly every 15 minutes throughout the day.

items: **Cleveland Hopkins International Airport:** the line's southwest terminus, on the city's far west side
**Ohio City:** near-west side station, in the neighborhood home to the West Side Market
**Tower City (Downtown):** the system's central rail hub, where the Red Line meets the Blue, Green, and Waterfront light-rail lines
**University Circle:** east-side stop serving the University Circle institutions, including Case Western Reserve University and the area's museums and hospitals

sections.h2: How much does it cost to rent a house near the Red Line?
body: Rent across our current Cleveland-area selection generally runs from about $700 to $1,800 a month, with a typical home around $1,000 — a range that holds up whether you're closer to the Ohio City end of the line or the University Circle end, since transit access isn't the main driver of price in our listings.

For broader market context, Zumper's report dated July 4, 2026 put Cleveland's overall median asking rent at $1,250 (up 4.2% year-over-year), while RentCafe's report dated July 2, 2026 found professionally managed apartments averaging $1,564. Both are citywide figures rather than Red Line-specific, since neither source breaks out rent by transit corridor.

rows: Most common size across our listings
Fits roommates or a small household

sections.h2: A car-optional commute beyond just the Red Line
body: Tower City isn't just the Red Line's midpoint — it's where the Blue Line and Green Line (both light rail to Shaker Heights) and the Waterfront Line (to the Flats and North Coast lakefront) all converge, so a home near any of those connecting lines still puts you within reach of the Red Line corridor with one transfer. The HealthLine, RTA's bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue, runs a separate but connected corridor between Public Square and University Circle, giving another car-optional option if your daily trip runs along that stretch instead.

If a fully car-optional commute matters, ask us specifically about proximity to a rail station or the HealthLine route rather than just "near the Red Line" — a five-minute walk to a station makes a bigger practical difference than being generally on the same side of the city.

sections.h2: Section 8 vouchers near the Red Line
body: Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers (Section 8) and is kept HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of how close it sits to the Red Line. If you're applying for a voucher for the first time, our [Cleveland Section 8 guide](/section-8-housing-cleveland-oh/) explains how CMHA's local application and inspection process works.

See our full current list of [houses for rent in Cleveland](/houses-for-rent-cleveland-oh/), or compare listings [near Downtown](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-near-downtown-cleveland/) and [near University Circle](/cleveland-rentals/houses/houses-for-rent-near-university-circle-cleveland/) if either end of the line fits your commute better.

faq.q: How often does the RTA Red Line run?
faq.a: Trains run roughly every 15 minutes throughout the day. The Red Line connects Cleveland Hopkins International Airport to Downtown's Tower City hub and continues to Windermere Station in East Cleveland, making it Greater Cleveland's only heavy-rail line and the only U.S. rapid-transit line linking a downtown directly to its main airport.

faq.q: Which neighborhoods does the Red Line serve?
faq.a: The route runs from Cleveland Hopkins Airport on the far west side through Ohio City, Downtown (Tower City), and University Circle on the east side, ending at Windermere Station in East Cleveland. It's the only U.S. rapid-transit line that connects a downtown directly to its city's main airport.

faq.q: Do you have houses near every Red Line station?
faq.a: We work with rental homes across Greater Cleveland, concentrated on the East and Southeast side, so proximity to a specific Red Line station depends on which stop and which listing. Tell us your target station or commute when you book a showing so we can match you with current options.

faq.q: Can I get to University Circle without a car?
faq.a: Yes. The Red Line serves University Circle directly, and RTA's HealthLine bus rapid transit route along Euclid Avenue also connects Public Square to University Circle, giving two separate car-optional routes to that area's hospitals, museums, and Case Western Reserve University.

faq.q: Can I use a Section 8 voucher on a house near the Red Line?
faq.a: Yes. Every home we work with accepts Housing Choice Vouchers and is HUD-inspection-ready, regardless of its distance from the Red Line or any other transit route. Check our Section 8 guide if you need help with the local CMHA application process.
